As someone currently studying and working in Australia, it's interesting that Rose's accent is considered sophiscated or elegant by non-Australians.  In Australia, Rose's accent would be considered quite common and is referred to as the general accent. There's three main types of accent, with variations in between the three: broad, general and cultivated. The different accents people have largely depends on which parts of Australia they live in. The general accent, which Rose has, is more widely found in Melbourne (where Rose is from), Canberra, Adelaide, Perth and Hobart. The broad accent, which is more nasally or hardcore bogan and considered less sophiscated, is more widely found in rural parts of Australia, as well as parts of Sydney and Queensland. Felix and Chan from Straykids, who are both from Sydney, speak with the broad accent.  The cultivated accent, which is considered more cultured/educated or elitist, is spoken by people like Cate Blanchett and Geoffrey Rush, both also from Melbourne.
